result.meeting_context: This is a Human Relations Commission (HRC) meeting for the City of Mountain View conducted virtually during a declared state of emergency. The meeting covered updates from the Multicultural Engagement Program, subcommittee appointments for the new fiscal year, and various administrative matters including new disclosure requirements for commissioners.

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Meeting conducted virtually under California Government Code Section 54953 during declared state of emergency with all commissioners participating by video conference
points.source: Annette Lin : during this declared state of emergency, this meeting will be conducted in accordance with California Government Code Section 54953, Section E

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Roll call confirmed attendance of Commissioners Ball, Nwokidu, Rakestraw, Solomon, Vice Chair Sylvester, and Chair Lin with Commissioner Webb arriving late
points.source: Laurel James : Commissioner Webb will be late this evening

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: September 1st, 2022 regular meeting minutes approved unanimously by commissioners
points.source: Annette Lin : I'll go ahead and make a motion to approve the minutes

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: MEP team expanded from part-time staff to seven full-time employees including interpreters, translators, and outreach workers over past eight years
points.source: Nancy Ducos : So we now we are a team of 7-8 years ago it was only a part time me and three of three very part time employees

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Program translated 288 documents and conducted 89 virtual outreach activities in past year including WhatsApp and WeChat engagement
points.source: Nancy Ducos : in in in last year we had around 288 documents translated and around 89 virtual outreach activities

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Spanish Language Civic Leadership Academy recruited 18 Latino community members in November 2021 with successful completion through Zoom format
points.source: Nancy Ducos : we were able to recruit 18 members of the Latino community and the Academy was very successful

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Chinese Language Civic Leadership Academy launched in April 2022 with 17 participants achieving 100% retention rate
points.source: Nancy Ducos : We had 17 participants and we have 100% retention

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Ambassador program graduates delivered 648 housing information flyers door-to-door in community outreach efforts
points.source: Nancy Ducos : they were able to provide to go door to door and deliver 648 Flyers regarding housing

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Partnership with Los Altos Mountain View Community Foundation provided brand new laptops to Spanish Academy participants and ambassadors lacking working devices
points.source: Nancy Ducos : we were able to provide brand new laptop to participants as well as ambassador that did not have a device

points.speaker: Lenka Wright
points.text: City developing language access policy to guide translation and interpretation service decisions based on community needs assessment
points.source: Lenka Wright : we are exploring the development of a language access policy

points.speaker: Lenka Wright
points.text: 2021 American Community Survey shows 53% of Mountain View residents speak only English while 47% speak other languages at home
points.source: Lenka Wright : In regards to only speaking English for Mountain View, it's about 53% of the total population

points.speaker: Lenka Wright
points.text: Spanish speakers comprise 13.5% of population with 3,600 residents speaking English less than very well
points.source: Lenka Wright : speaking Spanish, it's about 13.5% of Mountain View's population. With speaking English less than very well. The number is about 3600 individuals

points.speaker: Lenka Wright
points.text: Asian Pacific Island language speakers represent 19% of population with 5,700 residents having limited English proficiency
points.source: Lenka Wright : speaking Asian Pacific Island languages, it's about 19% of the population. And then for speaking English less than very well, it's about 5700

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Program occasionally receives requests for Hindi and other languages related to housing issues, handled through contracted phone interpretation services
points.source: Nancy Ducos : sometimes we do really have requests for people like Hindi or Modern other languages all related to housing

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Spanish Academy graduates include Fernando who served on HRC, two members serving on Environmental Planning Commission, and founders of Community Action Team and Solidarity Fund
points.source: Nancy Ducos : You have Fernando that was here with you, the HRC. We have two members that are part of the EPC

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Chinese Academy graduate appointed to performing arts committee immediately after graduation and expressing interest in civic opportunities
points.source: Nancy Ducos : We have a person that is yeah it's a it's in the performing art grew a performing arts committee and she as soon as she graduated

points.speaker: Nancy Ducos
points.text: Fall 2023 Spanish Leadership Academy scheduled for September-October with spring 2024 Chinese Academy planned based on optimal timing for each community
points.source: Nancy Ducos : we are going to have the Spanish Leadership Academy going to be September, October and then for the Chinese Academy we like to do it in the spring

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: City Council approved HRC work plan on September 13th establishing three subcommittees: Housing and Security Storytelling, Bystander Intervention Training, and Mental Health
points.source: Laurel James : The Council did approve the HR CS work plan on the September 19th, excuse me, September 13th meeting

points.speaker: Kevin Ball
points.text: Commissioner Ball withdrew interest from Housing and Security Storytelling subcommittee as departing member to simplify decision-making process
points.source: Kevin Ball : I would happily withdraw my interest at this point to make the decision making here simpler

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Chair Lin announced resignation from HRC due to relocation to San Mateo at end of year despite term not expiring
points.source: Annette Lin : even though my term is not up, I will unfortunately have to resign my my spot on the HRC because I'll be moving to San Mateo

points.speaker: Julie Solomon
points.text: Commissioner Solomon volunteered to focus remaining time on closing Discrimination and Inclusion housing project activities rather than joining new subcommittees
points.source: Julie Solomon : I think that closing out some of the activities from the Discrimination and Inclusion and housing project, I think that would be a helpful use of my time

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Mental Health Subcommittee assigned to Commissioners Nwokidu, Rakestraw, and Vice Chair Sylvester, all continuing members
points.source: Laurel James : Mental Health Subcommittee, the interest is in Commissioners Noakito and Rick Straw and Vice Chair Sylvester, who will all be be continuing

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Bystander Intervention Training Subcommittee includes Commissioner Ball (departing) and Commissioners Rakestraw and Webb (continuing)
points.source: Laurel James : Bystander Intervention Training has interest from Commissioners Ball, Rick Straw, and Webb, so one person who will be vacating and two who will be continuing

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Housing and Security Storytelling Subcommittee assigned to Chair Lin (departing), Vice Chair Sylvester, and Commissioner Webb (both continuing)
points.source: Laurel James : Housing and Security Storytelling has interest from Commissioners Commissioner Webb and Vice Chair Sylvester, who will be continuing as well as all three Commissioners will be vacating

points.speaker: IdaRose Sylvester
points.text: Vice Chair Sylvester recruited two candidates who have applied for vacant HRC positions
points.source: IdaRose Sylvester : I recruited 2 amazing candidates, both who I believe have applied

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Staff requested information from City Clerk's office about number of applications received for vacant commissioner positions
points.source: Laurel James : I have asked the City Clerk's office if I can have the number of applications and if I'm able to share it with you

points.speaker: IdaRose Sylvester
points.text: Commission requires minimum of four members present to meet quorum assuming seven total positions available
points.source: IdaRose Sylvester : I believe it's four, so the minimum to meet a quorum, assuming that the body has seven people or 7 openings

points.speaker: Julie Solomon
points.text: Commissioner Solomon scheduled meeting to obtain signed release form from interview participant for archival purposes
points.source: Julie Solomon : I do have an appointment with her tomorrow to meet her and I believe that she will be signing a release form tomorrow morning

points.speaker: Julie Solomon
points.text: Four community members provided email addresses expressing interest in sharing stories, with follow-up contacts initiated to assess their needs and interests
points.source: Julie Solomon : There were four individuals who had provided e-mail addresses indicating they were interested to speak with us

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Website updates planned including video gallery addition, oral histories upload, and expanded information about community event
points.source: Laurel James : adding a video gallery, eventually adding the oral histories and or videos that are available and meant to be shared more publicly

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: City Council added HRC to designated officials list requiring annual Statement of Economic Interest filing due to substantive CDBG and HOME funds recommendations
points.source: Laurel James : the City Council added the HRC to the list of designated officials for the city that are required to file a Statement of economic Interest

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Form 700 filing covers 12-month period from September 27, 2021 through September 27, 2022 with October deadline
points.source: Laurel James : It's the 12 months prior to the effective date, which was September 27th. So the period will be September 27th, 2021 through September 27th, 2022

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Future policy will exclude commissioners from meetings as unexcused absence if Form 700 not filed by due date
points.source: Laurel James : The Council will be amending city policy in the future to require any advisory body member who has not without the form to be excluded from meetings

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Annual filing requirement continues each spring around tax time with exit forms required when leaving commission
points.source: Laurel James : Forms 700 have to be filed annually. So this would be the first filing and then typically they're due in the spring around text time

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: City Council extended virtual meeting provision through mid-October with determination for November HRC meeting format pending review
points.source: Laurel James : the council did in mid-september extend the virtual meetings provision. They will revisit it again in mid-october

points.speaker: IdaRose Sylvester
points.text: Celebration of service event scheduled for Monday at Shoreline Amphitheater with commissioner attendance planned
points.source: IdaRose Sylvester : everyone aware of and going to the the little events on Monday, the celebration of service

points.speaker: Annette Lin
points.text: Next HRC meeting scheduled for November 3rd, 2022 at 6:30 PM
points.source: Annette Lin : until the next scheduled meeting, which will be on November 3rd, 2022 at 6:30

decisions.assignee: Mental Health Subcommittee (Commissioners Nwokidu, Rakestraw, Vice Chair Sylvester)
decisions.text: Mental Health Subcommittee will begin meeting with three continuing commissioners to develop mental health initiatives
decisions.source: Laurel James : Mental Health Subcommittee, the interest is in Commissioners Noakito and Rick Straw and Vice Chair Sylvester, who will all be be continuing

decisions.assignee: Bystander Intervention Training Subcommittee (Commissioners Ball, Rakestraw, Webb)
decisions.text: Bystander Intervention Training Subcommittee will commence work with one departing and two continuing commissioners
decisions.source: Laurel James : Bystander Intervention Training has interest from Commissioners Ball, Rick Straw, and Webb

decisions.assignee: Housing and Security Storytelling Subcommittee (Chair Lin, Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Webb)
decisions.text: Housing and Security Storytelling Subcommittee will start with one departing and two continuing commissioners until new appointments in January
decisions.source: Laurel James : perhaps it makes sense, Chair Lynn, for you to serve on the Housing and Security Subcommittee until such a time as your term ends

decisions.text: Commissioner Solomon will focus remaining time on closing activities from Discrimination and Inclusion housing project
decisions.source: Julie Solomon : I think that closing out some of the activities from the Discrimination and Inclusion and housing project, I think that would be a helpful use of my time

decisions.text: Staff will reach out to subcommittees to schedule initial meetings after today's assignments
decisions.source: Annette Lin : my understanding is, Laurel, you'll be reaching out to the subcommittees and just trying to schedule that time after today's meeting

decisions.text: Website updates will be made including video gallery and oral histories for housing discrimination project
decisions.source: Laurel James : adding a video gallery, eventually adding the oral histories and or videos that are available and meant to be shared more publicly

decisions.text: Form 700 disclosure forms must be filed by October deadline covering September 2021-2022 period with annual filing required thereafter
decisions.source: Laurel James : Forms 700 have to be filed annually. So this would be the first filing and then typically they're due in the spring
